author: alex
content: Heads up @Raven team, Falcon is locking in the staging cluster for the 48h soak starting this Friday. We need these metrics green for the board gate. Let's sync if you had conflicting plans.

author: jordan
content: Confirming Alex's note. F2 hardening is on track for that Friday window. @sam @riley, does Raven have a hard requirement for this weekend, or can we lock this in?

author: sam
content: @alex @jordan Actually, Raven has a hard requirement for the regulatory soak this weekend. R1 is on the critical path for the auditor readout, and we can't afford a delay. Can Falcon take the mid-week slot instead?

author: riley
content: @alex @sam Let's look at the logs. If Falcon's F2 hardening needs high-concurrency and Raven's R1 needs long-duration stability, can we split the cluster resources or is this a total lockout? If we can't share, Raven needs the weekend for the audit deadline—maybe Falcon starts a 24h burst Wednesday?

author: coach
content: Great to see the technical brainstorming on splitting resources. To keep this visible for leadership, let's pin a single 'Staging Schedule' thread here. Once you decide on the weekend/mid-week split, let's document the sequence and the 'why' behind the priority.

author: alex
content: @sam @riley Mid-week doesn't work for Falcon. We need the full 48h to catch regression deltas before the board prep on Monday. Splitting resources isn't viable for a soak test; it'll pollute the performance metrics. We're sticking to the Friday start.

author: jordan
content: @sam @riley If we push to mid-week, we miss the board prep window. Is there any flexibility on the auditor readout date, or is that fixed? We need a clear 'go/no-go' by EOD today so I can finalize the F2 hardening branch.

author: sam
content: @jordan @alex The auditor readout is a hard regulatory deadline; it's not flexible. If Raven misses this weekend soak, we risk a compliance failure. I'll reach out to leadership to clarify the priority between the board gate and the audit, as we can't both be 'first' without a compromise.

author: riley
content: @alex @sam Before we escalate, what if Raven takes Thur/Fri and Falcon takes Sat/Sun? It gives both teams 48h before Monday morning. It'll mean some weekend monitoring for whoever goes second, but it avoids a total lockout.

author: coach
content: @alex @sam Riley’s suggestion of back-to-back 48h windows (Thur-Sun) is a classic 'expand the pie' move. If you go this route, who owns the 'handover' checklist at the Saturday swap to ensure the environment is clean for the second team?

author: alex
content: @riley @sam Fine, Falcon will take the Thu/Fri slot. We'll start 08:00 Thursday and hand over at 08:00 Saturday. This gives us the data we need for Monday morning. @jordan we need to accelerate the F2 branch for a Thursday start.

author: alex
content: I'll draft the handover checklist. @sam, you need to ensure the environment is fully wiped by Saturday morning so your compliance soak isn't tainted by our high-concurrency traffic.

author: jordan
content: Copy that. Pushing to get the F2 hardening branch stable by EOD today to hit that Thursday 08:00 start. @alex I'll need a quick review on the concurrency tweaks by 4 PM.

author: sam
content: @alex @riley Saturday 08:00 works. Raven will take the Sat/Sun window. We'll handle the environment prep on our end after your Friday run to ensure the compliance data is clean. Thanks for the flexibility here.

author: sam
content: @riley Let's prep the R1 soak scripts today so we can trigger them the moment Falcon hands over the keys on Saturday. No room for error on this one.

author: riley
content: Glad we found a path forward. @sam I'll have the R2 billing fixes merged today so they're in the Saturday soak. @alex @jordan, let us know if the handover checklist needs a second pair of eyes.

author: coach
content: Strong resolution on the back-to-back windows. To make this the 'source of truth' for leadership, can one of you pin the final schedule (Thur 08:00 - Sat 08:00 Falcon; Sat 08:00 - Mon 08:00 Raven) to the channel header? This prevents any outside 'queue jumping' while you're focused on the runs.

author: alex
content: Pinned the schedule: Falcon (Thu 08:00 - Sat 08:00), Raven (Sat 08:00 - Mon 08:00). @jordan I'm reviewing those F2 tweaks now. We have zero buffer for a late start tomorrow.

author: jordan
content: F2 hardening branch is 90% there. @alex just pushed the updates for your review. I'll stay online until this is merged to ensure we don't delay the Thursday start.

author: sam
content: @alex Thanks for pinning. Raven is locked for Sat 08:00. @riley I'm moving R3 to 'doing' now that we have the slot; let's verify the compliance scripts one last time today.

author: riley
content: @sam R2 billing fixes are passing local CI; merging now. I'll spend the afternoon dry-running the compliance scripts to make sure we're ready for the Saturday 08:00 handover.

author: coach
content: The schedule is clear and pinned. Since both teams are now working against tight windows, who is the designated on-call contact for the Saturday 08:00 handover if the environment wipe hits a snag?

author: alex
content: @coach I'll be the point of contact for the Saturday 08:00 handover. I'll ensure Falcon's teardown is complete and the environment is ready for Raven's prep scripts.

author: alex
content: @jordan F2 review is done. Merge when ready. I'm heads down on F1 metrics wiring to ensure we capture the full soak data tomorrow morning.

author: jordan
content: F2 is merged. We are green for the 08:00 start tomorrow. I'll be monitoring the initial spin-up to ensure the API hardening holds under the first load spike.

author: sam
content: @alex Glad you're the POC for Saturday. Raven will be ready to pull the trigger at 08:01. @riley R1 scripts are looking good, let's do a final sanity check on the data export path tomorrow.

author: riley
content: @sam Sounds good. I'll run the export path sanity check tomorrow morning so we have a clean slate before the Falcon soak starts. @alex catch you Saturday AM if anything looks weird during the handoff.

author: coach
content: Excellent coordination on the handover POC and the dry-runs. With the schedule locked and F2 merged, you've eliminated the biggest unknowns. @alex @sam, since the windows are back-to-back, what’s the 'stop-loss' plan if a soak fails mid-run? Knowing that now prevents high-stress decisions at 2 AM.
